description:
secretx-keyring/src/lib.rs: KeyringBackend's `Send + Sync` bounds come transitively from `SecretStore: Send + Sync` (secretx-core/src/lib.rs:1240) — the impl will not compile if a future field breaks autoderive. So the safety property is enforced indirectly.

A defensive contributor would add an explicit `static_assertions::assert_impl_all!(KeyringBackend: SecretStore + WritableSecretStore + Send + Sync);` in tests. This is belt-and-suspenders; Rust already enforces it via the trait impl. Filing as an opinion-tier finding because the engineering benefit is marginal: the cost of a future field-addition that breaks Send/Sync is a compile error at the impl site, which is exactly where the contributor would be working.

Filed as opinion. Could be closed without action.
title: No compile-time assertion that KeyringBackend: Send + Sync (relies on trait bounds)
